data.content:
### API Authentication

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v1/authentication.mdx

All API requests must be made over HTTPS and authenticated using an API key provided as a query parameter.

```APIDOC
## API Authentication

### Description
Authentication to the API is performed via URL Query Params. Provide your API key as a query param.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v1/

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **apiKey** (string) - Required - Your Cal.com API key. Test mode keys start with `cal_` and live mode keys start with `cal_live_`.

### Request Example
```
https://api.cal.com/v1/?apiKey=cal_live_xxxxxx
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - A success message indicating authentication.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Authentication successful"
}
```

#### Error Response (401)
- **message** (string) - An error message indicating authentication failure.

#### Error Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Invalid API key"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### V1 API Authentication Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Demonstrates how to authenticate API requests in Cal.com V1 using an API key in the query string. This method is deprecated.

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v1/event-types?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx
```

--------------------------------

### API Authentication Header Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx

Demonstrates how to authenticate API requests using an Authorization header with a Bearer token. This is a common pattern for many REST APIs.

```http
'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'
```

--------------------------------

### API Authentication Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/apps/api/v1/README.md

This example demonstrates how to authenticate API requests using an apiKey query parameter. The apiKey is generated from the Cal.com app settings and must be included in every request.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v1/users?apiKey={INSERT_YOUR_CAL.COM_API_KEY_HERE}
```

--------------------------------

### V2 API Authentication Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Shows the recommended way to authenticate API requests in Cal.com V2 using a Bearer token in the Authorization header for enhanced security.

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v2/event-types \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_xxxxxx"
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com API v1 to v2 Authentication

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/v1-v2-differences.mdx

Demonstrates the shift in authentication methods from API v1's query parameter to API v2's Authorization header. API v2 also introduces the 'cal-api-version' header for version control.

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v1/bookings?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx
```

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_test_xxxxxx" \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13"
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com API Authentication v1 vs v2

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/v1-v2-differences.mdx

Demonstrates the authentication method change between Cal.com API v1 and v2.

```APIDOC
## Authentication Changes

### API v1 Authentication
API keys are passed as a query parameter.

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v1/bookings?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx
```

### API v2 Authentication
API keys are passed in the `Authorization` header using the `Bearer` scheme. The `cal-api-version` header is also required.

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_test_xxxxxx" \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13"
```
```

--------------------------------

### API Authentication

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/apps/api/v1/README.md

The Cal.com API requires a valid API key to be passed as a query parameter (`apiKey`) for all requests. API keys can be generated in the Cal.com settings.

```APIDOC
## API Authentication (API Keys)

The API requires a valid `apiKey` query parameter to be passed.

**Example:**
```sh
GET https://api.cal.com/v1/users?apiKey={INSERT_YOUR_CAL.COM_API_KEY_HERE}
```

API Keys can optionally have expiry dates and must be associated with a `userId` to function correctly. Future support for Header Bearer Auth may be added.
```

--------------------------------

### OAuth Client Credentials Header Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx

Shows how to authenticate API requests using OAuth client credentials, which include a client ID and a secret key passed in custom headers. This is specific to Cal.com's API.

```http
x-cal-client-id: YOUR_CLIENT_ID
x-cal-secret-key: YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com v2 API Authentication Methods

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/faq.mdx

The Cal.com v2 API supports two primary authentication methods. You can use a Bearer token for user-specific access or client credentials for OAuth client authentication. Ensure you use the correct header format for each method.

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://api.cal.com/v2/...
```

```bash
curl -H "x-cal-client-id: <client-id>" -H "x-cal-secret-key: <client-secret>" https://api.cal.com/v2/...
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All OAuth Clients for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all OAuth clients associated with a managed organization via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication is performed using the managed organization's API key.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients
Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>
```

--------------------------------

### Create OAuth Client for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates a new OAuth client for a managed organization using a POST request to the Cal.com API. Requires the managed organization's API key for authentication. Accepts client name, redirect URIs, and permissions.

```json
{
    "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
    "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"] ,
    "permissions": ["*"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### V1 vs V2 Authentication

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

This section details the differences in authentication methods between Cal.com API V1 and V2.

```APIDOC
## Authentication Changes

### V1 Authentication
In V1, you authenticated using an API key in the query string:

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v1/event-types?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx
```

### V2 Authentication
In V2, authentication uses header-based API keys for improved security:

```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v2/event-types \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_xxxxxx"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Specific OAuth Client for Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves details of a specific OAuth client for a managed organization using its ID via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication requires the managed organization's API key.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients/:oAuthClientId
Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>
```

--------------------------------

### Get Event Types

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v1/introduction.mdx

This endpoint allows you to list all of your event types. An API key is required for authentication.

```APIDOC
## GET /v1/event-types

### Description
Retrieves a list of all event types associated with your account.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
https://api.cal.com/v1/event-types

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **apiKey** (string) - Required - Your Cal.com API key for authentication.

### Request Example
```bash
curl https://api.cal.com/v1/event-types?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **type** (object) - Description of an event type (details not provided in source).
- **name** (string) - The name of the event type.
- **slug** (string) - A unique identifier for the event type.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "event_type_1",
      "title": "30 Minute Meeting",
      "slug": "30-minute-meeting",
      "description": "A 30 minute meeting slot.",
      "duration": 30
    },
    {
      "id": "event_type_2",
      "title": "1 Hour Call",
      "slug": "1-hour-call",
      "description": "A 1 hour call.",
      "duration": 60
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Managed User - Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/global-event-types.mdx

Creates a new managed user within the Cal.com platform. Requires the user's 'email', 'name', and 'timeZone'. The response includes essential user details such as 'id', 'accessToken', and 'refreshToken', which should be securely stored for future authentication and management.

```json
{
    "email": "charlie@gmail.com",
    "name": "charlie",
    "timeZone": "Europe/Rome"
}
```

```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "user": {
            "id": 1458,
            "email": "charlie+clxyyy21o0003sbk7yw5z6tzg@gmail.com",
            "username": "charlie-clxyyy21o0003sbk7yw5z6tzg-gmail-com",
            "name": "charlie"
        },
        "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoiYWNjZXNzX3Rva2VuIiwiY2xpZW50SWQiOiJjbHh5eXkyMW8wMDAzc2JrN3l3NXo2dHpnIiwib3duZXJJZCI6MTQ1OCwiZXhwaXJlc0F0IjoxNzQ1NDkwODQwMDAwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDU0ODcyNTh9.FI9-Hi7O_j4QLn7SuNJlOnmk-0MHhZoXPCncsmgyCoQ",
        "accessTokenExpiresAt": 1745490840000,
        "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoicmVmcmVzaF90b2tlbiIsImNsaWVudElkIjoiY2x4eXl5MjFvMDAwM3Niazd5dzV6NnR6ZyIsIm93bmVySWQiOjE0NTgsImV4cGlyZXNBdCI6MTc3Njk4MTYwMDAwMCwiaWF0IjoxNzQ1NDg3MjU4fQ.YYTH7WeshMR-BpbNeSYFUubjTlsAsOhdhrge-LngRiw",
        "refreshTokenExpiresAt": 1776981600000
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Refresh Managed Organization API Key (POST)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Use this endpoint to refresh the API key for a managed organization. You must include your current API key in the Authorization header. The request body allows you to specify the validity period for the new API key or set it to never expire. The response contains the newly generated API key.

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.cal.com/v2/api-keys/refresh \
-H "Authorization: Bearer <your_current_api_key>" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"apiKeyDaysValid": 30}'
```

```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "apiKey": "cal_xxx467709fffebe8c6559211cb5f178f"
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /v2/me

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Retrieve the profile of the authenticated user in V2.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/me

### Description
Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`/v2/me`

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the user.
- **username** (string) - The username of the user.
- **email** (string) - The email address of the user.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "user_xyz789",
  "username": "johndoe",
  "email": "john.doe@example.com"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com Google Calendar Callback URIs

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/apps/install-apps/google.mdx

These are the authorized redirect URIs required for the Google OAuth flow within Cal.com. They specify where Google should send the user back after authentication. Ensure `<Cal.com URL>` is replaced with your Cal.com instance's domain.

```text
<Cal.com URL>/api/integrations/googlecalendar/callback
<Cal.com URL>/api/auth/callback/google
```

--------------------------------

### Create Booking API (V2 Example)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Example of creating a booking using the V2 API, including authentication and request body structure.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings

### Description
Create a new booking.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings

### Request Body
- **eventTypeId** (integer) - Required - The ID of the event type.
- **start** (string) - Required - The start time of the booking in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z').
- **attendee** (object) - Required - Information about the attendee.
  - **name** (string) - Required - The attendee's name.
  - **email** (string) - Required - The attendee's email address.
  - **timeZone** (string) - Required - The attendee's time zone (e.g., 'America/New_York').

### Request Example
```json
{
  "eventTypeId": 123,
  "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
  "attendee": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the operation (e.g., 'success').
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created booking.
  - **id** (integer) - The booking ID.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique booking identifier.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the booking.
  - **startTime** (string) - The start time in ISO 8601 format with milliseconds.
  - **endTime** (string) - The end time in ISO 8601 format with milliseconds.
  - **status** (string) - The booking status (e.g., 'accepted').
  - **attendees** (array) - List of attendees.
  - **metadata** (object) - Additional metadata about the booking.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 123,
    "uid": "cal_abc123xyz",
    "title": "Meeting",
    "startTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
    "endTime": "2024-01-15T11:00:00.000Z",
    "status": "accepted",
    "attendees": [...],
    "metadata": {...}
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Event Types using Cal.com API V1 and V2 (Python)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Demonstrates how to fetch event types from the Cal.com API using both V1 and V2. V1 utilizes query parameters for authentication, while V2 uses an Authorization header. Ensure you replace placeholder API keys with your actual credentials. V1 is scheduled for discontinuation on February 15, 2026.

```python
import requests

# V1 example
response_v1 = requests.get(
    'https://api.cal.com/v1/event-types',
    params={'apiKey': 'cal_test_xxx'}
)
event_types_v1 = response_v1.json()['event_types']

# V2 example
response_v2 = requests.get(
    'https://api.cal.com/v2/event-types',
    headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer cal_live_xxx'}
)
data_v2 = response_v2.json()

event_types_v2 = []
if data_v2['status'] == 'success':
    event_types_v2 = data_v2['data']
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Managed Organization by ID via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a specific managed organization using its ID via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication is done using the manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com Environment Variable for Google Credentials

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/apps/install-apps/google.mdx

This environment variable stores the downloaded JSON content of your Google OAuth Client ID. It is essential for Cal.com to authenticate with Google services. Ensure the entire JSON string is pasted correctly.

```dotenv
GOOGLE_API_CREDENTIALS=<JSON_CONTENT_HERE>
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All OAuth Clients

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all OAuth clients associated with the authenticated managed organization.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/oauth-clients

### Description
Fetches a list of all OAuth clients created for the authenticated managed organization.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.cal.com/v2/oauth-clients`

### Parameters
#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token using the managed organization's API key (e.g., `Bearer cal_xxx168ceb89117cc3a1b1f1777511506`).

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- An array of OAuth client objects, each containing 'clientId', 'name', 'redirectUris', and 'permissions'.

#### Response Example
```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "clientId": "xxxepq3bg0000khghtq4bxxx",
            "name": "OAuth client for managed organization",
            "redirectUris": ["https://your-domain.com*"],
            "permissions": ["*"]
        }
    ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All Managed Organizations via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Retrieves a list of all managed organizations associated with a manager organization via a GET request to the Cal.com API. Authentication requires the manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret.

```http
GET https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com API Managed User Creation Response Structure

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/quickstart.mdx

This JSON structure represents a successful response from the Cal.com API when creating a managed user. It contains a status, user details, and authentication tokens (access and refresh) that should be stored for future use.

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": 179,
      "email": "bob@example.com",
      "username": "bob"
    },
    "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoiYWNjZXNzX3Rva2VuIiwiY2xpZW50SWQiOiJjbHUxZmNtYjEwMDAxa2hyN3g3ZHJleWw0Iiwib3duZXJJZCI6MTc5LCJpYXQiOjE3MTE0NDI3OTR9.EsC3JRPHQnigcp_HSijKCIp8EgcWs2kj4AFxYXYc9sM",
    "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0eXBlIjoicmVmcmVzaF90b2tlbiIsImNsaWVudElkIjoiY2x1MWZjbWIxMDAwMWtocjd4N2RyZXlsNCIsIm93bmVySWQiOjE3OSwiaWF0IjoxNzExNDQyNzk0fQ.GjklEucgey8yWMoGz7ABntbxYdiqqQFPooQjqGd3B5I"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Updates an existing managed organization using a PATCH request to the Cal.com API. Allows modification of the organization's name or metadata. Authentication is handled with the manager organization's OAuth client credentials.

```http
PATCH https://api.cal.com/v2/organizations/:organizationId/organizations/:managedOrganizationId
Authentication: x-cal-client-id: <OAuth client id>
x-cal-secret-key: <OAuth client secret>

{
    "name": "updated organization name",
    "metadata": {
        "new_key": "new_value"
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Managed Organization via Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx

Creates a new managed organization using a POST request to the Cal.com API. Requires manager organization's OAuth client ID and secret for authentication. Accepts organization name, optional metadata, and API key validity period.

```json
{
    "name": "some organization",
    "metadata": {
        "some": "metadata"
    },
    "apiKeyDaysValid": 60
}
```
